delivery_goal: Deliver identity atoms and state vocabularies required by all later components.
rationale: IDs and State are universal dependencies; behavior-bearing components need them first.

delivery_goal: Deliver read-only projections, report/evidence carriers, and diagnostic taxonomy.
rationale: Every production-functional component needs visibility and evidence before behavior grows.

delivery_goal: Deliver capacity envelopes, scaling profile vocabulary, and safe-point guards.
rationale: Capacity determines container shape; safe-points guard reset, scaling, eviction, and invalidation.

delivery_goal: Deliver slot/container residency structures and indexed container registry.
rationale: Runtime coordination needs stable residency structures before waiting lists, rounds, and refs.

cluster_name: Topology, Moderation, Waiting Lists, And Rounds
delivery_goal: Deliver container family topology, per-container moderation, eligibility lists, and round coordination.
rationale: These components coordinate containers after registry/residency exists.

delivery_goal: Deliver reference supply, ingress placement surface, and dispatch output exposure.
rationale: Movement execution depends on waiting lists, rounds, registry, slot/container state, and boundary-safe refs.

delivery_goal: Deliver release/recycle, eviction behavior, and communication boundary contracts.
rationale: These depend on prior state/reference/movement/exposure models and protect lifecycle/boundary behavior.

delivery_goal: Deliver post-bridge/read-side circulation profile, including retry, backpressure, delayed release, receiver/addon boundaries.
rationale: Read-side profile depends on dispatcher exposure, communication boundaries, lifecycle, capacity, diagnostics, and receiver/addon contracts.
